overview.description: The posterior cervical corridor provides access to the dorsal cervical spine, posterior fossa-cervical junction, and lateral masses. Approaches range from midline laminectomy and laminoplasty to lateral mass and pedicle screw fixation. Key anatomical considerations include the nuchal ligament, vertebral arteries at C1-2, and the cervical facet joints and lateral masses.

approaches.name: Posterior Cervical Laminectomy & Fusion
approaches.desc: Midline approach with laminectomy for decompression and lateral mass or pedicle screw fixation for stabilization. For multi-level stenosis with instability or kyphosis.

approaches.name: Laminoplasty
approaches.desc: Open-door or French-door technique to expand the spinal canal while preserving posterior elements. Motion-preserving option for multi-level cervical stenosis with maintained lordosis.

approaches.name: C1-2 Fusion (Goel-Harms)
approaches.desc: C1 lateral mass screws with C2 pedicle or pars screws for atlantoaxial instability. Requires careful identification of the vertebral artery groove on C1 and the C2 pedicle anatomy.

approaches.name: Posterior Cervical Foraminotomy
approaches.desc: Keyhole laminotomy for lateral disc herniations or foraminal stenosis. Motion-preserving alternative to ACDF for unilateral radiculopathy with lateral pathology.

anatomy.sub: Posterior cervical musculature, lamina, lateral masses, C1-2 anatomy, vertebral artery V3 segment, cervical pedicles

radiology.cases: Multi-level cervical stenosis, atlantoaxial instability, cervical facet dislocation, OPLL, os odontoideum

orPearls.imagingReview: Evaluate sagittal alignment (C2-7 SVA, lordosis), cord compression and signal change, facet integrity, and vertebral artery course — especially V3 segment on CTA for C1-2 surgery. Assess pedicle size on axial CT for screw planning.
orPearls.setup: Prone, Mayfield pins, neck in neutral to slight flexion. Avoid hyperflexion which can worsen cord compression. Confirm levels with fluoroscopy. For C1-2 fixation, palpate the C1 lateral mass and identify the C2 pedicle entry point under fluoroscopy.

decisions.label: Laminectomy vs Laminoplasty?
decisions.val: Laminoplasty for multi-level stenosis with maintained lordosis and no instability. Laminectomy with fusion when kyphosis, instability, or need for fixation exists.

decisions.label: Lateral Mass vs Pedicle Screws?
decisions.val: Lateral mass screws (Magerl technique) for subaxial spine — safe, reliable. Pedicle screws offer stronger fixation at C2 and C7, but higher VA risk in subaxial spine.

decisions.label: C1-2: Pars vs Pedicle at C2?
decisions.val: Pedicle screws provide strongest fixation. Pars screws (isthmus screws) are an alternative when vertebral artery anatomy makes pedicle screws risky — shorter trajectory, lower VA risk.

decisions.label: Extent of Fusion?
decisions.val: Fuse the minimum segments needed. Extending to occiput significantly restricts motion. Consider standalone C1-2 fusion when possible rather than occipitocervical fusion.
